author: @dokham18
text:
As someone whose childhood was He-Man, Thundercats and Transformers and the 1987 MOTU film being the first film I ever watched at the cinema as a kid, this is my most anticipated movie of the year purely from a nostalgia point of view. It's nice to see a measured critique from you as many other reactions are hyperbolic one way or the other. I winced too at the Earth-related stuff and the "He/Him" thing is definitely a joke imo. The Eternia they've built looks incredible though.

I'm expecting the film to have heart, adventure, escapism and humour akin to Chris Pine's Dungeons & Dragons. Travis Knight did really well with Bumblebee and Kubo and it looks like he's paid reverence to the cartoon in a very literal way - for better or worse depending on what your preferences are. The initial responses from test screenings are positive so hoping it'll be a fun summer movie that people enjoy and one that 40-somethings like me can be sent back to our childhood days in front of the TV for those 2 hours.
